exactly when to stop generating text

and returns the output up to that point—even if it hasn't hit

Generation stops immediately, output is returned up to that point

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# Model will stop when it generates "."
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # May never reach this
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Stop at first period
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Tell me about Python"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: "Python is a programming language."
# (Stops at first period, even if max_tokens=1024)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["\n\n", "END", "###"],  # Stop on any of these
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a story"}]
)

# Model stops if it generates:
# - Double newline (结束的 paragraph)
# - "END"
# - "###"
No stop sequence (model stops naturally at end of turn or when

When no stop sequence is set, the model:
- Continues until it naturally completes the response
- Or stops when

limit is reached
- Or stops due to

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Stop at first period
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: "The capital of France is Paris."
# (Stops at first period, concise)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],  # Stop at paragraph break
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: Single paragraph (no double newline)
# (Prevents multi-paragraph responses)
responses for UIs that display limited text.[]

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["```"],  # Stop if code block starts
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Return JSON: {name: 'Alice', age: 30}"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# Output: '{"name": "Alice", "age": 30}'
# (Stops before generating markdown code block)
without extra markdown or text spillover.

system_prompt = """
You are a helpful assistant. Format your responses like this:

Assistant: [your response]
"""

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system=system_prompt,
    stop_sequences=["\nHuman:", "\nAssistant:"],  # Stop at next conversation turn
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# Output: "Assistant: Hello! How can I help you today?"
# (Stops before next "Human:" or "Assistant:" label)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["END"],  # Stop when "END" appears
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a short story. End with 'END'"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# Output: Story text (stops before "END" string)
# (Ensure model includes "END" in prompt, but doesn't repeat it)
6. List Item Limit (Stop After N Items)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["\n4."],  # Stop after 3 items (before 4th)
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "List 3 benefits of Python"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# Output:
# 1. Easy to learn
# 2. Great libraries
# 3. Large community
# (Stops before generating "4.")

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["```yaml", "```python", "```"],  # Stop at any code block marker
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write YAML config"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: YAML content (stops before markdown code block)
# (Prevents markdown wrapping)

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Stop at first period
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AI?"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: "AI is artificial intelligence."
# (Stops at first period)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["\n\n", "END", "###"],  # Multiple stop points
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a short poem"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: Single paragraph poem (stops at first \n\n or END or ###)
Example 3: Stop Sequence with System Prompt

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    system="You are a concise assistant. Give one-sentence answers.",
    stop_sequences=["\n"],  # Stop at newline
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is Python?"}]
)

print(message.content.text)
# Output: "Python is a programming language."
# (Single line, no newline)
Example 4: Stop Sequence for Structured Output

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["```"],  # Stop before markdown code block
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Return JSON: {'name': 'Alice'}"}]
)

print(message.content[0].text)
# Output: '{"name": "Alice"}'
# (Raw JSON, no markdown)

stream = client.messages.stream(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],  # Stop at paragraph break
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain AI in one paragraph"}]
)

with stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end="", flush=True)
    
    print()
    final_message = stream.get_final_message()
    print(f"Tokens: {final_message.usage.output_tokens}")
❌ Mistake 1: Stop Sequence Appears Too Early

# ❌ WRONG — stop sequence in prompt appears immediately
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Period in prompt
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Tell me about AI."}]  # Period here!
)

# Output: Empty (stops immediately at prompt's period)

# ✅ CORRECT — stop sequence not in prompt
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Stop at period in output only
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Tell me about AI"}]  # No period
)

# Output: "AI is artificial intelligence."
# (Stops at period in output)
❌ Mistake 2: Using Stop Sequence with max_tokens

# ❌ RISKY — redundant if max_tokens is too small
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=5,  # Too small for meaningful response
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Won't matter if max_tokens hits first
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AI?"}]
)

# Output: "AI is"  # Stopped at max_tokens, not stop sequence

# ✅ CORRECT — max_tokens high enough for stop sequence to work
message = client.messages.create()
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # High enough
    stop_sequences=["."],  # Stops at period
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AI?"}]
)

# Output: "AI is artificial intelligence."
# (Stopped at stop sequence)

# ❌ WRONG — case mismatch
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["END"],  # Uppercase
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a story. End with 'end'"}]
)

# Output: Story continues past "end" (doesn't match "END")

# ✅ CORRECT — case matches exactly
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    stop_sequences=["end"],  # Lowercase (matches output)
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a story. End with 'end'"}]
)

# Output: Story stops at "end"
Check if stop sequence is in prompt or too common

# ❌ WRONG — period in prompt causes early stop
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["."],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AI?"}]  # No period
)

# ✅ CORRECT — use less common stop sequence
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],  # Less common than period
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is AI?"}]
)
Issue 2: Model Doesn't Stop at Sequence

# ❌ WRONG — extra space
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=[" END "],  # Extra spaces
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "End with 'END'"}]
)

# ✅ CORRECT — exact match
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["END"],  # Exact
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "End with 'END'"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD — enforce single-sentence answers
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["."],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is Python?"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD — system prompt + stop sequence
message = client.messages.create(
    system="You are concise. Give one-sentence answers.",
    stop_sequences=["\n"],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is Python?"}]
)
3. Use Multiple Stop Sequences for Safety

# ✅ GOOD — multiple stop points
message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["\n\n", "END", "###"],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a short story"}]
)

# ✅ GOOD — test first
test_message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Test: Explain AI"}]
)
print(test_message.content[0].text)  # Verify it works

# Then use in production
prod_message = client.messages.create(
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain AI"}]
)

# ✅ BEST — both stop sequence AND max_tokens
message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,  # Safety ceiling
    stop_sequences=["\n\n"],  # Smart stop at paragraph
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Explain AI"}]
)

# Stops at "\n\n" (smart) OR 1024 tokens (safety)
